author: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon
book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.

metadata.author: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon

title: Canon IV.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Let those who truly and sincerely lead the monastic life be counted worthy of becoming honour; but, forasmuch as certain persons using the pretext of monasticism bring confusion both upon the churches and into political affairs by going about promiscuously in the cities, and at the same time seeking to establish Monasteries for themselves; it is decreed that no one anywhere build or found a monastery or oratory contrary to the will of the bishop of the city; and that the monks in every city and district shall be subject to the bishop, and embrace a quiet course of life, and give themselves only to fasting and prayer, remaining permanently in the places in which they were set apart; and they shall meddle neither in ecclesiastical nor in secular affairs, nor leave their own monasteries to take part in such; unless, indeed, they should at any time through urgent necessity be appointed thereto by the bishop of the city.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: And no slave shall be received into any monastery to become a monk against the will of his master.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: And if any one shall transgress this our judgment, we have decreed that he shall be excommunicated, that the name of God be not blasphemed.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: But the bishop of the city must make the needful provision for the monasteries.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Ancient Epitome of Canon IV.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Domestic oratories and monasteries are not to be erected contrary to the judgment of the bishop.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Every monk must be subject to his bishop, and must not leave his house except at his suggestion.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: A slave, however, can not enter the monastic life without the consent of his master.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Like the previous canon, this one was brought forward by the Emperor Marcian in the sixth session, and then as number one, and the synod accepted the Emperor’s proposed canon almost verbally.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Occasion for this canon seems to have been given by monks of Eutychian tendencies, and especially by the Syrian Barsumas, as appears from the fourth session.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: He and his monks had, as Eutychians, withdrawn themselves from the jurisdiction of their bishops, whom they suspected of Nestorianism.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Here observe (1) the definite assertion of episcopal authority over monks, as it is repeated for greater clearness in the last words of the canon, which are not found in Marcian’s draft, “It is the duty of the bishop of the city to make due provision for the monasteries,” and compare canons 8, 24.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Isidore says that the bishop must “keep an eye on the negligences of monks” (Epist., i.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: The Western Church followed in this track (see Council of Agde, canon xxvii., that “no new monastery is to be founded without the bishop’s approval,” and Ist of Orleans, canon xix., “Let abbots be under the bishop’s power,” and also Vth of Paris, canon xij., Mansi, viii., 329, 354, 542, etc.), until a reaction set in against the oppressiveness of bishops, was encouraged by Gregory the Great (Epist., i.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: This canon, cut up and mutilated, is found in the Corpus Juris Canonici, Gratian’s Decretum, Pars II., Causa XVI., Quæst.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: II., Canon X.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: I have followed the reading of the Prisca, and of Dionysius, of Routh, and of Balsamon, “they were set apart,” i.e. (as Balsamon explains) where they received the monastic tonsure.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: This reading substitutes ἀπετάξαντο for ἐπετάξαντο , which would mean “over which they had been put in authority,” or possibly (as Johnson) “where they are appointed,” or as Hammond, “in which they have been settled.”

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Isidore reads “ordinati sunt.”
